prior.content_md:
**Registration Mechanism**

All BREEAM New Construction V7 projects must be registered by a licensed BREEAM New Construction Assessor via the new BREEAM Platform (platform.breeam.com). The previous BREEAM Projects portal remains in use for older scheme versions but V7 registrations are exclusively processed through the new platform. Registrations are non-refundable once a registration number has been issued. The building type must be correctly classified before registration is completed, as this cannot be changed post-registration.

Only licensed NC assessors who have completed the NC V7 Version Update Training Module (approximately 4 hours, delivered via BRE Academy) are eligible to register V7 assessments. Existing licensed assessors are automatically enrolled onto the Academy module; new assessors must complete full NC licensing training before they can register projects.

**UK vs. International Scope**

BREEAM UK New Construction V7 applies to commercial projects in England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland. BREEAM International New Construction V7 applies in all other countries unless a local national adaptation scheme exists. For US-based workspace assessors, a US-specific International NC training pathway is available via BRE Academy. For projects in Canada (CA) and across the Americas, the International NC V7 Technical Manual governs unless a national adaptation has been approved.

**Data Fields Required at Registration**

The following data must be collected and confirmed before registration: (1) asset location and jurisdiction (determines UK vs. International scheme); (2) building type / use class (determines applicable category and fee scale); (3) net floor area in numerical format (commas and decimal points not accepted in the system field); (4) target rating (Pass, Good, Very Good, Excellent, Outstanding) — this determines which minimum standards and LCA obligations apply from concept stage; (5) EU Taxonomy alignment pathway — decision made at registration and cannot be retroactively added; (6) confidentiality preference — if the project is marked confidential it will not appear on GreenBookLive once certified.

**Mixed-Use Assets**

V7 consolidates mixed-use buildings under a single certificate and scheme registration, reducing the complexity previously associated with multi-asset-type projects. This is directly relevant for workspace facilities that combine freight handling, fine art storage, and office or client reception functions within a single envelope.

**BREEAM In-Use and RFO**

BREEAM In-Use remains at Version 6 as of 2026. V7 editions of Refurbishment & Fit-Out and In-Use are in development; specific transition plans have not yet been published. Workspaces occupying existing certified facilities should monitor BRE announcements for the In-Use V7 transition timeline.

*Source: BREEAM Knowledge Base — Registrations, BRE Global, updated 25 March 2026. https://kb.breeam.com/wp-content/plugins/breeamkb-pdf/pdf/?c=920; BREEAM New Construction V7 FAQs, BRE Global, 2025–2026. https://breeam.com/breeam-newconstructionv7/breeam-version-7-faqs; BREEAM — Prepare to Register Your First Project, BRE Global, 2025. https://breeam.com/breeam-newconstructionv7/prepare-to-register-your-first-project; BREEAM Knowledge Base — Who Can Register V7, BRE Global, 2025. https://kb.breeam.com/knowledgebase/who-can-register-v7-new-construction-assessments-on-the-breeam-platform/*
